steps.title: Map the two halves of the passage
steps.content: Sentences 1–3 describe what the researchers did and found: they used JWST to measure wavelengths through WASP-39b's atmosphere and detected CO₂. The final sentence pivots to significance — the finding is the first decisive evidence of CO₂ in an exoplanet atmosphere and signals the JWST's broader potential.

steps.title: Name the overall structure
steps.content: The passage moves from a scientific discovery to its importance — discovery, then significance.

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It discusses a method used by some researchers, then states why an alternative method is superior to it.: no alternative method is introduced or compared. ✗
  • Choice B — It describes how researchers made a scientific discovery, then explains the importance of that discovery.: matches the discovery-then-significance structure exactly. ✓
  • Choice C — It outlines the steps taken in a scientific study, then presents a hypothesis based on that study.: the closing sentence states implications, not a new hypothesis to be tested. ✗
  • Choice D — It examines how a group of scientists reached a conclusion, then shows how other scientists have challenged that conclusion.: no challenge or counter-research is mentioned. ✗
That matches Choice B — It describes how researchers made a scientific discovery, then explains the importance of that discovery..
